name: Rich Mullins
title: Christian Singer-Songwriter
tagline: Beloved Christian songwriter remembered for ‘Awesome God’ and a life of public faith
bio: Richard Wayne Mullins was an American contemporary Christian singer-songwriter best known for songs such as ‘Awesome God’ and ‘Sometimes by Step.’ His work left a deep mark on late twentieth-century evangelical music, and he became known not only for strong songwriting but also for unusual seriousness about discipleship, repentance, simplicity, and devotion to Christ. Mullins died in 1997, and his legacy has remained firmly identified with Christian worship and witness.
faith: Publicly available biographical material presents Mullins as a Christian artist whose life and work remained explicitly tied to Christian belief through the end of his life. He was baptized, wrote and performed overtly Christian songs, and is remembered as one of the most spiritually serious figures in Christian music of his era. While his path included ecclesial complexity, the available record does not indicate a departure from Christian faith. Under the clarified SDG standard for deceased unmatched artists, he is a strong fit for Historical biographies.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Rich Mullins
sources.note: Biographical overview identifying Mullins as a contemporary Christian musician and documenting his Christian background and public career
